commit.message:
feat(m1): Hash crate WASM + FFI bindings (milestone 1.1) (#4)

* feat(m1): add WASM + FFI bindings for hash crate (milestone 1.1)

- Make experimentation-core optional dep in hash crate (fixes WASM compilation
  blocker from chrono/uuid being pulled into wasm32 target)
- Add wasm_is_in_allocation() binding alongside existing wasm_bucket()
- Add experimentation_is_in_allocation() to FFI crate with u8 return for C ABI
- Harden FFI experimentation_bucket() with null-pointer checks and
  EXPERIMENTATION_BUCKET_ERROR sentinel (u32::MAX) instead of UB
- Add include guard (EXPERIMENTATION_FFI_H) to generated C header
- Add lib crate-type to FFI crate to enable Rust integration tests
- Create WASM test vector validation (10K vectors via include_str, wasm32 gated)
- Create FFI test vector validation (10K vectors through C ABI + null safety)
- Add wasm-pack test and FFI test CI steps
- Fix missing workspace deps: pyo3, assert_approx_eq, tonic-web, tch,
  wasm-bindgen-test
- Fix pre-existing murmur3 test_known_values wrong expected for seed=1

Unblocks: Agent-7 (CGo hash bridge), web SDK (WASM bindings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix counters never recording in SQLOverview PSModule

Add-Counter used $script:polymon but $polymon was set as a plain local
variable — inside a module function, $script: refers to the module scope
not the function's local scope, so the condition was always false and
$Counters.Add() was never called. Changed all references to $script:polymon
to match $script:errlvl which already used the correct pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: PolyMon
repository.description: Modernized fork of PolyMon project.  Powershell based network and device monitoring and alerting

commit.message:
Performance optimizations - 13 fixes implemented

Client-side:
- Colony lookup cache (O(1) instead of O(n))
- Warning deduplication optimization (store base message, eliminate string parsing)
- Fix Array.erase() in loops (contracts, market events) - prevents O(n²)
- Worker/ship availability caches with dirty flags
- Mission leg count optimization (avoid filter().size())
- Invalidate caches on hire/fire/purchase operations

Server-side:
- Add database indexes (mission, worker, stockpile, ship queries)
- Fix N+1 queries on mission fetch (add eager loading)

Performance gains:
- Colony lookups: O(n) → O(1)
- Warning checks: 100+ string ops → 1 comparison
- Contract/event removal: O(n²) → O(n)
- Database queries: 100+ → 3 (via eager loading)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix taxonomy search: use ACF AJAX endpoint instead of empty select options

The select element has no options because ACF taxonomy fields use
AJAX to fetch terms from the database. Reading selectElement.options
always returned empty.

Rewrite to:
- Hit acf/fields/taxonomy/query AJAX endpoint with field_key + search
- Debounce input (250ms) to avoid hammering the server
- Show "Searching..." feedback while loading
- Create <option> elements dynamically when a term is selected
- Dispatch change event so ACF tracks the value for save

commit.message:
Opcja B: per-person godziny w assignments (estimatedHours + actualHours)

Zmiany typów:
- TaskAssignment: estimatedHours (planowane, PM) + actualHours (wykonane, zespół)
- Task: usunięto globalne estimatedHours (zastąpione sumą z assignments)

Migracja (v3): konwersja starych assignments {personId} → {personId, estimatedHours, actualHours}

Panel zadania - sekcja "Przypisane osoby":
- Każda osoba ma osobne pola: Planowane [h] + Wykonano [h]
- Przycisk 🔢 (kalkulator) auto-oblicza z dat zadania i dostępności osoby:
    workingDays(start, end) × (weeklyHours / 5)
  z tooltipem pokazującym wyliczenie przed kliknięciem
- Podgląd kosztu: ≈ estimatedHours × stawka (przed, obok Planowane)
                  = actualHours × stawka (po wykonaniu, obok Wykonano)
- Pola "Planowane" edytuje PM (canEdit), "Wykonano" edytuje zespół (canProgress)

commit.message:
Fix CI build: switch Tailwind from Vite plugin to PostCSS

@tailwindcss/vite fails in GitHub Actions (Linux) due to a Vite bug
where ESM resolution doesn't traverse out of node_modules/.vite-temp/.
Switching to @tailwindcss/postcss avoids the issue entirely.

Three fixes for test-windows.yml failures:

- Pin WiX dotnet tool to v5.0.2 (v6.0.2 produced corrupt MSI) and add
  post-build file size verification
- Skip file permission assertion in TestApply_DownloadsAndReplaces on

fix(ego-lint): add src/ metadata.json fallback for build-system extensions (#24)

## Summary

Extensions using a `src/` layout (meson build system) keep
`metadata.json` in `src/` rather than the extension root. This PR adds
fallback lookup to all 7 scripts that read metadata.json:

- `ego-lint.sh` (file-structure check)
- `check-metadata.py`
- `check-schema.sh`
- `apply-patterns.py` (`_get_shell_versions()`)
- `check-lifecycle.py` (2 functions: unlock-dialog comment, lockscreen
signals)
- `check-quality.py` (`get_session_modes()`)
- `check-disclosures.py`

Without this fix, src/ layout extensions get `metadata/exists` FAIL,
`file-structure/metadata.json` FAIL, and all version-gated rules
silently SKIP.

Closes #18

## Test plan

- [x] New fixture: `src-metadata@test` with metadata.json only in `src/`
- [x] Assertions verify: PASS for `file-structure/metadata.json` with
`(in src/)`, no `metadata/exists` FAIL
- [x] `bash tests/run-tests.sh` passes (513 assertions)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make migration non-fatal to handle existing schema

previously created. Make migration step non-fatal so the app starts

handle signals.

fix: skip .storybook/ files from Angular plugin transform

The Angular compiler's output for .storybook/preview.ts causes Rollup
parse errors on CI ("Legacy octal escape" in strict mode). These files
have no Angular decorators, so esbuild handles them directly. Also
removed temporary debug logging and moved the Windows casing fix out
of the platform-specific guard so the .storybook skip applies everywhere.

commit.message:
feat(m2): implement event pipeline with rotating Bloom filter dedup (#1)

Delivers milestones 1.6 (IngestExposure + IngestMetricEvent RPCs),
1.7 (IngestRewardEvent + IngestQoEEvent RPCs), and 1.8 (Bloom filter
dedup optimization with hourly rotation and Prometheus metrics).

Key changes:
- Activate proto types via tonic::include_proto! for all 8 packages
- Full event validators (exposure, metric, reward, QoE) with fail-fast
- Idempotent Kafka producer (enable.idempotence=true, acks=all, lz4)
- Rotating Bloom filter: current + previous, hourly rotation, ~7.5MB/filter
- Prometheus metrics: items_current, items_previous, rotations_total, duplicates_total
- Metrics HTTP endpoint on port 9090 (configurable via METRICS_PORT)
- 29 tests passing (validation, dedup, rotation, FPR bounds, Prometheus)

What this unblocks:
- Agent-3 (metric computation): events on exposures + metric_events topics
- Agent-4 M4b (bandit rewards): events on reward_events topic

feat: implement entity memory engine with knowledge graph support

Entity engine provides auto-extracted knowledge graph functionality:
- Entity CRUD with type validation (person, org, location, concept, event, other)
- Alias resolution prioritizing canonical name over aliases
- Relationship management with direction filtering
- Mention tracking with source references and automatic counts
- Entity merging preserving aliases, relationships, and attributes
- Extraction queue for async entity extraction pipeline
- Semantic search across entity summaries via embeddings
- Query operation for combined entity/relationship/mention retrieval

Includes comprehensive test coverage (28 tests).
